assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as a webhook compatibility problem when upgrading Kubernetes versions and explains that webhooks are blocking pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides specific commands: 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks' to identify failing webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Option 1 explicitly offers bypassing the webhook temporarily by deletion, and Option 2 offers disabling it for specific namespaces

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Provides exact commands: 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook <webhook-name>' and 'kubectl delete mutatingadmissionwebhook <webhook-name>'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: While the response mentions this is temporary and provides multiple options, it doesn't explicitly warn about the security/validation implications of removing the webhook

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions checking cert-manager compatibility and provides a link to the supported releases documentation

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides detailed upgrade commands using helm to upgrade cert-manager to v1.14.x for Kubernetes 1.32 compatibility (note: expectation says 1.30 but context is 1.32)

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to set failurePolicy to 'Ignore' as an alternative approach

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --dry-run=server' command to test pod creation

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: While the response shows helm upgrade commands, it doesn't explicitly mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems to survive redeployments

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Shows how to set failurePolicy to Ignore but doesn't explicitly warn about the risks or recommend reverting to Fail after fixing the root cause
